field.label: Enabled
field.help: Bring this connection up. Only one PharosVPN client should be enabled at a time (single awg interface).

field.label: Interface
field.help: The tun interface name (awg0..awg999). The plugin joins it to the 'pharosvpn' group and, from the LAN sources below, generates the gateway, outbound NAT, policy-route and kill-switch automatically.

field.label: Profile (.pharos)
field.help: The imported .pharos profile blob (base64). Use the Import button to upload a file; encrypted-mode payloads stay sealed at rest.

field.label: Profile password
field.help: Password for a password-mode profile (stored so the daemon can open it unattended on reboot). Leave blank for none/account-mode profiles.

field.label: Connection
field.help: Which named connection config inside the bundle to use. Populated from the imported profile.

field.label: Entry node
field.help: Which entry node to dial. Leave on the cascade entry / first hop unless you know otherwise.

field.label: Routing mode
field.help: Full = default route through the tunnel. Split = only the profile's AllowedIPs. None = let pf policy routing decide (recommended on a firewall, see LAN sources below).

field.label: LAN sources to route
field.help: LAN networks/hosts to policy-route through the tunnel (e.g. 192.168.1.0/24). These get an outbound-NAT + route-to-gateway pass (and a kill-switch block if enabled); only these sources are routed — the firewall's own / management traffic is never touched. Leave empty to route nothing automatically. Do NOT list the management subnet or you may lock yourself out.

field.label: Kill-switch
field.help: Fail closed: block the routed LAN sources from leaking to the WAN if the tunnel drops.
